growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Morgan Stanley said AI demand is driving a worsening DRAM shortage and expects memory prices to rise at least 25% from Q2 to Q3 2026, while WSJ highlighted long-term take-or-pay contracts making future AI memory revenue unusually visible.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Micron combines a cheap forward multiple with a confirmed AI memory shortage and multi-year HBM/DRAM pricing power.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ServiceNow is already monetizing agentic AI at enterprise scale, with Q1 cRPO at $12.64 billion, Now Assist customers spending over $1 million growing more than 130% year over year, and a July 22 earnings call now in focus for another AI revenue update.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ServiceNow has a near-term earnings catalyst, strong cRPO growth, and real enterprise AI monetization at a reasonable forward multiple for its growth.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: FIS has been cut roughly in half from its high yet still trades at about 8.1x earnings with an Aug. 4 print as the next hard catalyst; if the market has over-discounted execution risk, the valuation can re-rate, but payments competition and slower spending remain the bear case.
growth_picks.analyst_take: FIS is deeply discounted with strong FCF, cheap earnings multiples, and an August 4 earnings catalyst, despite integration and leverage concerns.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Morningstar still marks Microsoft as significantly undervalued, while recent sell-side commentary says heavy AI capex and the July 29 earnings date have compressed the stock despite intact cloud growth.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Microsoft's valuation has reset while Azure, Copilot, and AI infrastructure demand remain intact, but capex scrutiny keeps it below STRONG BUY.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: A director bought about $500K of stock while the shares were still deeply out of favor, and follow-up coverage says CRM is the worst-performing Dow name in 2026 with a large buyback now in play.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Salesforce has a rare combination of large FCF, cheap forward valuation, insider buying, and aggressive buybacks after a major sentiment washout.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: NATO's July 7, 2026 announcement to buy up to five MQ-4C Triton aircraft directly increases Northrop's ISR backlog and reinforces demand for maritime surveillance as alliance tensions stay elevated.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Northrop has a cleaner defense-space/ISR catalyst stack than most peers, with NATO Triton demand and a discounted share price supporting upside.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Counterpoint Mutual Funds opened a new $4.21M stake in Leidos while the company posted a strong quarter and raised FY2026 EPS guidance, which is the kind of institution-plus-fundamental setup that can matter.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Leidos combines a deep drawdown, cheap forward multiple, raised guidance, and defense IT demand into one of the cleaner value setups in the batch.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Regeneron combines strong operating results with a fresh repurchase authorization and an August 2026 FDA catalyst for garetosmab, giving it a more measured but still meaningful binary event setup.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Regeneron offers quality biotech cash flow at a reasonable multiple with a defined August FDA catalyst for garetosmab.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: IREN signed $2.8 billion of new multiyear AI cloud contracts, lifted its year-end AI cloud revenue target above $4 billion, and said customer prepayments will cover a large share of GPU capex, making the neocloud pivot look materially de-risked.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Huge AI cloud contract wins and customer prepayments de-risk the pivot, but negative FCF, leverage, and extreme beta keep this below STRONG BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Hut 8 finalized a 15-year, $9.8 billion lease at Beacon Point in Texas, doubling a tenant's committed capacity to 704 MW and pushing the campus toward full commercialization, which turns the company into a contracted AI data-center landlord rather than a speculative miner remnant.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Beacon Point commercialization turns Hut 8 into a contracted AI data-center infrastructure story, though financial quality is still early and volatile.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Barclays upgraded Lumentum on the view that AI networking demand and laser shortages are still tightening the market, with management saying products are sold out through 2027 and margin gains already showing up in the numbers.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI optical demand, laser shortages, and sold-out capacity support earnings upside, but the stock already prices in a lot of success.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Fiserv is about 69% below its 52-week high and only about 10% above its low, but the CEO exit has already triggered insider buying and fresh strategic-interest chatter ahead of its Aug. 6 earnings release; the bear case is that the payments reset proves structural rather than temporary.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Fiserv is a messy turnaround, but insider buying, cheap valuation, and activist pressure create a credible contrarian setup.

catalysts_general: Discovery: UHS is 38.6% below its 52-week high, still only about 8% above the low, and reports on Jul. 27; the setup is a defensive healthcare name priced for reimbursement and labor risk, but the 6.3x P/E leaves room if margins stabilize.
growth_picks.analyst_take: UHS is a low-multiple defensive healthcare reset with near-term earnings upside, but labor and reimbursement risks cap conviction.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: HII is 41.3% below its high and within 8% of the low, with Jul. 30 earnings as the near-term checkpoint; the upside is defense backlog and shipbuilding execution, while program delays or budget noise are the bear case.
growth_picks.analyst_take: HII offers a discounted defense backlog story with a July 30 earnings catalyst, but shipbuilding execution risk is persistent.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Goldman’s consumer-experience theme highlights Disney, and fresh analyst notes still see upside even as the stock trades near its lows and sentiment remains cautious.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Disney is a reasonable contrarian consumer-experience play with streaming, parks, buybacks, and analyst support, though linear media remains a drag.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Comcast combines a low valuation with a near-52-week-low setup and an earnings date on July 23 where options imply a move, creating a classic contrarian reset candidate.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Comcast is deeply out of favor and cheap with FCF support, a July 23 earnings catalyst, and asset-separation optionality.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: The July 2026 NATO summit produced new European co-production and maintenance commitments for Lockheed, including ATACMS production work in Germany and a PAC-3 maintenance facility in Europe, extending missile demand tied to the Russia-Ukraine war and NATO rearmament.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lockheed has missile-demand catalysts from NATO rearmament and PAC-3 expansion, though growth, leverage, and valuation are less compelling than peers.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Chevron signed Iraqi agreements on July 17, 2026 that include new production and a pipeline project aimed at reducing dependence on the Strait of Hormuz, giving it direct upside from regional energy-security spending and alternative export routes.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Chevron offers a cash-generative energy-security setup as Iraq pipeline and production deals could add geopolitical optionality without balance-sheet stress.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Energy Fuels received conditional U.S. government support in July 2026 to accelerate rare-earth and critical-materials processing, a direct policy-backed beneficiary of the U.S. effort to rebuild domestic industrial capacity.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Energy Fuels has a rare policy-backed critical-minerals catalyst, but negative FCF and weak margins keep this speculative.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Two directors bought aggressively over the past two weeks: Bruce Taylor acquired 700,000 shares for $7.9M and Jay Pack bought 226,000 shares for $2.55M after AVO pulled back about 24% from its April peak, a strong board-level conviction signal.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Large insider purchases after a pullback create a credible turnaround signal, though avocado pricing and revenue decline make sizing important.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Nextpower completed the Prevalon Energy acquisition and launched an advanced energy-storage business aimed at grid-connected storage, hybrid power, and AI data-center applications, which expands it from solar hardware into the power stack AI builds need.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Nextpower's Prevalon deal gives the solar tracker story a broader grid-storage and AI data-center power angle at a reasonable forward multiple.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: BR is still 44.9% below its high and only about 12% above the low, with a near-term earnings window estimated for Aug. 4-7; if recurring-revenue durability shows up, the drawdown can repair, but this one is more timing-sensitive because the catalyst date is estimated rather than formally announced.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Broadridge is a durable recurring-revenue compounder trading like a problem stock, with enough earnings support to justify buying weakness.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Morningstar says Lululemon is trading far below its fair value estimate, and its recent commentary suggests the market may be over-discounting near-term sales pressure.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Lululemon looks statistically cheap for a high-margin brand, but the buy case depends on stabilizing guidance after recent product and Americas weakness.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: China's rare-earth export controls and the White House's July 20, 2026 critical-minerals order keep MP Materials at the center of U.S. de-risking from Chinese supply, even though the group is also dealing with near-term oversupply fears.
growth_picks.analyst_take: MP is the rare-earth name with the strongest policy support, aided by Pentagon investment and price-floor economics despite sector oversupply fears.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Director Chad Deaton bought 35,000 shares for $173,250 on July 7, aligning with improving backlog and fresh contract wins; it is a smaller purchase, but it came from a board member at a $5.65B company with operating momentum.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Transocean offers a leveraged offshore-cycle setup with backlog growth, insider buying, positive FCF, and short-squeeze optionality.

catalysts_general: Discovery: QXO saw 51,527 options contracts trade with 10,783 July 17 $23 calls accounting for 21% of volume; Barchart also highlighted a $2.868M/$2.4M long-call ratio spread, pointing to a bullish multi-leg bet around Brad Jacobs' consolidation story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: QXO is a speculative Brad Jacobs roll-up with large upside if integration works, but negative FCF and high short interest demand small sizing.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Snowflake is turning AI into a measurable growth lever, with its CEO's compensation now tied to $100 billion of added shareholder value and analysts still modeling near-30% sales growth for fiscal 2027 as the company pushes purpose-built AI products.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Snowflake is executing well on AI data products, but valuation, near-high price action, and insider selling weaken the entry setup.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Datadog's observability platform is monetizing AI infrastructure growth, with the stock up sharply in 2026, revenue guidance lifted to as high as $4.34 billion, and a recent Adaptive ML acquisition intended to deepen AI research and monitoring capabilities.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Datadog is a high-quality AI observability compounder, but the valuation and target-price gap argue against chasing it here.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Freeport is benefiting from a fresh copper pullback-and-rebound setup, and Goldman’s M&A-candidate framing keeps it on the radar as a high-beta materials play tied to AI infrastructure and electrification.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Freeport has copper leverage and AI/electrification tailwinds, but Grasberg execution risk and only moderate upside keep it from being a fresh buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: The CEO bought just over $504K of stock, and analysts have been raising targets on improving spread dynamics, so this is a cleaner insider-backed industrial/value idea.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Commercial Metals has credible insider support and cheap valuation, but modest upside and a recent EPS miss make the entry less compelling.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: RTX said on July 7, 2026 that Raytheon is doubling global Stinger production and expanding its European supplier base, a direct beneficiary of NATO's push to harden short-range air defense capacity.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Defense demand and Stinger production expansion are real, but valuation and limited target upside leave little margin of safety.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Institutional buyers stepped in across multiple filings: Sound Shore Management initiated a $70.66M position, Adams Diversified Equity Fund added $7.5M, and Independent Financial Group added $3.62M, signaling broad buy-side accumulation in a battered industrial blue chip.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Boeing demand and certification momentum are improving, but leverage, execution history, and rich forward multiples prevent a clean buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: A 10% owner group bought more than $1.6M of stock in a cluster, and the company is still screening as a low-P/E, high-yield value name with management buybacks supporting the setup.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Energizer is optically cheap with insider buying, but extreme leverage and negative sales growth keep it from clearing the buy bar.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Royal Caribbean fits the travel-and-experience rotation and has been backed by fresh buy-side coverage even after a pullback, suggesting the market may be underestimating demand resilience.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Royal Caribbean has demand momentum and a near-term earnings catalyst, but high leverage and negative FCF argue against chasing.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: European gas prices jumped again in mid-July 2026 as US-Iran conflict threatened LNG flows through Hormuz, supporting U.S. LNG exporters like Cheniere that can redirect incremental supply into a tighter global market.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheniere is a high-quality LNG infrastructure beneficiary, but leverage and limited upside make it a hold after the run.

catalysts_general: Discovery: USA Rare Earth's July 2026 acquisition and CEO transition are aimed at strengthening a mine-to-magnet chain just as China keeps rare-earth export controls in place, making the stock a direct lever on allied supply-chain re-shoring.
growth_picks.analyst_take: USA Rare Earth has a timely mine-to-magnet story, but the Serra Verde deal and CEO transition leave too much execution risk for a buy today.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Barchart flagged unusually heavy bullish call activity, including almost 8,000 June 18 $450 calls trading more than 73x prior open interest and a separate $255 call line with volume over 63x open interest, which looks like directional conviction.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AMD's AI data-center execution is impressive, but after a huge run the stock needs a blowout Q2 to justify the multiple.

catalysts_general: Discovery: PPC sits 39.8% below its high but only 9.9% above the low, with a Jul. 30 earnings call and a sub-8x P/E; low float and 15.6% short interest can help if chicken spreads hold, but commodity volatility and disease headlines are the bear case.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Pilgrim's Pride is optically cheap, but weak earnings quality, negative FCF, limited target upside, and commodity risk make it a value trap candidate.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Kraft Heinz remains deeply discounted in Morningstar’s framework, and the ongoing breakup/split speculation gives it a potential rerating path if management execution improves.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Kraft Heinz is cheap, but weak guidance and the paused separation make it look more like a value trap than a timely turnaround.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Live Nation got a fresh UBS price-target hike today on strong live-event demand, and the stock is already trading near highs, so the news flow still looks supportive.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Live Nation has strong demand, but valuation, near-high pricing, and unresolved antitrust remedy risk make the risk/reward unattractive.
